shared_prefix:
You are part of Clanker Zone, a strict evidence-first review council.
Review only real defects.
Do not punish harmless CBIC formatting artifacts.
Treat hint-derived source blocks and raw HTML as provenance evidence.
Every confirmed issue must cite exact evidence and explain why it is a real defect.
If uncertain, return needs_manual_review instead of over-asserting.

Domain: GST rule extraction review.
Primary evidence hierarchy:
1. raw source meaning
2. hint-derived structured evidence
3. schema semantics
4. extracted rule JSON
Known source quirks:
- amendment markers can span multiple blocks and provisos
- CBIC spacing and punctuation are often ugly but legally harmless
- numbered table rows like (1) and (2) may still be header structure
- source_ref.anchor_text is a short UI anchor phrase, not a verbatim exact quote
- display_label may legitimately hold the clause/proviso label while text omits it
- raw_text may legitimately be null when source_ref is the authoritative audit anchor
- cross_ref.target_id may legitimately be null when deterministic resolution is unavailable
- effective_until on an omitted node can be valid chronology, not duplicate status
- enacted_date alone does not prove effective_from
Known non-issues unless stronger source evidence proves otherwise:
- anchor_text being shorter than the full source block
- anchor_text omitting a clause label like (b), (e), or (h)
- separate sibling nodes each carrying the same cross_ref when the source repeats the mention separately
- missing target_id for external_act references
- text omitting a legal label that is already preserved in display_label

Return only one of:
- confirmed_issue
- acceptable_artifact
- needs_manual_review
- no_issue
Return JSON only with this shape:
{
  "label": "...",
  "category": "...",
  "node_id": "...",
  "severity": "...",
  "title": "...",
  "problem": "...",
  "evidence_refs": ["..."],
  "recommended_fix": "...",
  "confidence": 0.0
}
The dossier may cover a whole sub-rule or clause cluster.
When you return a finding, node_id must point to the exact affected node from the dossier's target cards, not just the cluster root.
Severity must be exactly one of: critical, major, moderate, minor.
Use [] for evidence_refs when there is no concrete citation.
Use a numeric confidence. If uncertain, use 0.5 and downgrade the label instead of inventing certainty.
Use null where a field does not apply. Do not emit markdown.

The registration granted to a person is liable to be cancelled, if the said person, -

label.description: Machine identifier within parent. Rules: '36', '10B'. Sub-rules: '1', '4A'. Clauses: 'a'. Sub-clauses: 'i'. Tables: 'table-1'. Formulas: 'formula-1'.

type.description: Structural or qualifier node type for rule extraction.

text.description: Clean current text with amendment markers stripped. For a root rule this may hold the chapeau before children. For table/formula nodes it may hold the intro sentence or surrounding legal text. Required for content-bearing node types except pure structural container nodes whose children start immediately with no standalone chapeau text of their own.

raw_text.description: Original current text with inline amendment markers preserved when different from 'text'. For textless structural container nodes, raw_text must also be null; source_ref remains the authoritative audit anchor.

operative_text.description: Continuation text that appears after enumerated children in the same sentence.

children.description: Structural children only - sub-rules, clauses, sub-clauses, tables, and formulas.

explanations.description: Explanations and illustrations that qualify or define terms for this node.

cross_refs.description: Cross-references found in this node's text.

source_ref.description: Deterministic source citation span for this node. Authoritative for UI/source linking; raw_text may remain a node-local fragment.

amendment_markers.description: Links this node to numeric items in the top-level amendments array. Non-numeric markers like '*' remain top-level amendment entries only and must not appear here.

effective_from.description: When this current node version became effective.

effective_until.description: When this node version was superseded. Null means currently in force.

status.description: Current legal status of the node in the consolidated rules view.

evidence.label: XREF-DUPE-CGST-R21-f
evidence.text: [cross_refs] Duplicate cross-reference emitted for one source mention: The same cross-reference target has been emitted multiple times from the same textual mention.

evidence.label: CHRON-EFFECTIVE-MISSING-CGST-R21-b-1
evidence.text: [chronology] Node is missing effective_from despite hint having effective_date: The hint amendment for marker 1 has effective_date '2017-06-22', but the node has no effective_from.

evidence.label: CHRON-DATE-AMBIGUOUS-CGST-R21-b-2
evidence.text: [chronology] Amendment has enacted_date but no clear effective date: The hint amendment for marker 2 was enacted on '2020-12-22' but the source footnote does not specify a w.e.f. date. The node has no effective_from.

marker.description: Footnote identifier as rendered on the rule page. e.g. '1', '2', '*'.

text.description: Full footnote text exactly as on the source page.

commencement_date.description: ISO 8601 commencement date when distinct from effective_date.

enacted_date.description: ISO 8601 enacted date when the source gives only the amending Act date.

notification_number.description: Structured notification number extracted from footnote text.

notification_date.description: ISO 8601 date of the notification itself when extractable.

target.description: Textual selector of what was affected. e.g. 'sub-rule (4)', 'clause (b)'.

historical_text.description: Superseded text preserved in the footnote when a substitution note reproduces the prior wording.

events.description: Structured legal events parsed from this one footnote. Usually one event; compound source notes may contain multiple notifications/orders.

text.description: Source fragment that yielded this structured event.

effective_date.description: ISO 8601 effective date for this event when extractable.

commencement_date.description: ISO 8601 commencement date when distinct from effective_date.

enacted_date.description: ISO 8601 enacted/issuance date when only a bare dated reference is present.

instrument_type.description: Type of amending instrument for this event.

notification_number.description: Structured notification/order number for this event.

notification_date.description: ISO 8601 notification/order date for this event.

target.description: Optional event-specific textual selector of what was affected.

commencement_date.description: ISO 8601 commencement date when distinct from effective_date.

enacted_date.description: ISO 8601 enacted date when the source gives only the amending Act date.

notification_number.description: Structured notification number extracted from footnote text.

notification_date.description: ISO 8601 date of the notification itself when extractable.

target.description: Textual selector of what was affected. e.g. 'sub-rule (4)', 'clause (b)'.

historical_text.description: Superseded text preserved in the footnote when a substitution note reproduces the prior wording.

events.description: Structured legal events parsed from this one footnote. Usually one event; compound source notes may contain multiple notifications/orders.

text.description: Source fragment that yielded this structured event.

effective_date.description: ISO 8601 effective date for this event when extractable.

commencement_date.description: ISO 8601 commencement date when distinct from effective_date.

enacted_date.description: ISO 8601 enacted/issuance date when only a bare dated reference is present.

instrument_type.description: Type of amending instrument for this event.

notification_number.description: Structured notification/order number for this event.

notification_date.description: ISO 8601 notification/order date for this event.

target.description: Optional event-specific textual selector of what was affected.

evidence.label: Hint amendment 5
evidence.text: 5. Inserted (w.e.f. 01.10.2022) vide Notification No. 19/2022 - CT dated 28.09.2022.

payload.text: 5. Inserted (w.e.f. 01.10.2022) vide Notification No. 19/2022 - CT dated 28.09.2022.

candidate_fragment.text: 6. Inserted vide Notification No. 12/2024 - CT dated 10.07.2024.
